revision.text:
[[ja:伊勢命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|伊勢命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Isemikoto Shrine | native_name = 伊勢命神社 | image = 伊勢命神社.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isemikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.319754|133.235446}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Isemikoto Shrine'''|伊勢命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Oki Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11659043|ltq=Q11659043|qid=Q11659043|1=List of Shikinaisha in Oki Province}} ** Series ordinal: 15 ** P155: {{ill|Mizuwakasu Shrine|lt=Mizuwakasu Shrine|en|Mizuwakasu Shrine|ja|水若酢神社|simple|Mizuwakasu Shrine|zh|水若酢神社|qid=Q11549109|1=Mizuwakasu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041146|ltq=Q135041146|qid=Q135041146|1=Kasuga Shrine (Q135041146)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1205039|ltq=Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}} * {{ill|Oki Province|lt=Oki Province|cs|Provincie Oki|de|Provinz Oki|en|Oki Province|es|Provincia de Oki|fa|ولایت اوکی|fr|Province d'Oki|id|Provinsi Oki|it|Oki (provincia)|ja|隠岐国|ko|오키국|lt|Okio žemė|nl|Oki (provincie)|pt|Província de Oki|ru|Оки (провинция)|simple|Oki Province|sk|Oki (provincia)|sr|Провинција Оки|tr|Oki (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Окі|zh_min_nan|Oki-kok|zh|隱岐國|qid=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}} * {{ill|Q11597172|ltq=Q11597172|qid=Q11597172|1=Ochi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.319754|133.235446}} == image (P18) == * 伊勢命神社.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183006 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いせみことじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7280005005720 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県隠岐郡隠岐の島町久見字宮川原375', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:伊勢命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11378969}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Isemikoto Shrine |画像 = [[File:伊勢命神社.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}{{ill|lt=Okinoshima|Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}375 Aza Miyagawara, Kumi |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ise-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Myojin-tai) / former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Oki-zukuri|Q11659055|qid=Q11659055|1=Oki-zukuri}} copper-plate roof |別名 = Naigū-san |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|qid=Q2723|1=July 26}} |神事 = Kumi Kagura (July 26 or 25) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture|label=Isemikoto Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Isemikoto Shrine''' (いせみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Okinoshima|Q1205039|qid=Q1205039|1=Okinoshima, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Oki District|Q735735|qid=Q735735|1=Oki District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|qid=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' as "Isemikoto Shrine, Myojin-tai" in Ochi District of {{ill|lt=Oki Province|Q1063829|qid=Q1063829|1=Oki Province}}, and its modern-era rank was former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine is at the northern end of {{ill|lt=Dōgo|Q3623017|qid=Q3623017|1=Dōgojima}}. === Shrine name === From the '{{ill|lt=Onshū Shichō Gōki|Q11659057|qid=Q11659057|1=Inshū Shichō Gōki}}' of {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|qid=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|qid=Q7588|1=1667}}) onward, all writings call it "Naigū" or "Naigū Daimyōjin," and in the early modern period it came to be regarded almost as an offshoot of the {{ill|lt=Kōtaijingū|Q11581011|qid=Q11581011|lt=Inner Shrine|1=Kōtai Jingū}} of Ise. However, old tradition survived, including naming the deity "Ise-no-Mikoto," so in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} it reverted to "Isemikoto Shrine" following the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. === Enshrined deity === *'''Ise-no-Mikoto''' Although this deity is unique to Oki and not seen elsewhere, from the divine name and its relation to {{ill|lt=Isobe (be-min)|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe}} it is thought to be a deity deeply connected to the Ise region through maritime people'Nihon no Kamigami.'. There is also faith in its efficacy for {{ill|lt=rain prayer|UNKNOWN|lt=praying for rain}}'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku.'. === Origin === According to the transmission of the {{ill|lt=shake|Q11588367|qid=Q11588367|1=Shake (social class)}} family, when the Ise lineage first settled on Oki Island, a divine fire (mysterious fire) came nightly over the sea and stopped at Kariya, about 5.5 km southwest of the current site. They built a small {{ill|lt=hokora|Q1442984|qid=Q1442984|lt=shrine|1=Hokora}} there and enshrined their ancestral deity '''Ise Myōjin'''; the fire ceased, and that shrine was later moved to the present site. In the {{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|qid=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}, the '{{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=732|Q23569|qid=Q23569|1=732}}) Oki Province tax register' records Isobe no Atae Mantoku as assistant district governor of Yakidō (Ochi) District. Also, though in {{ill|lt=Dōzen|Q11476216|qid=Q11476216|1=Dōzen}}, a {{ill|lt=wooden tablet|Q4299994|qid=Q4299994|lt=Heijō-kyū mokkan|1=Mokkan}} bears the name Ishibe no Masaki of Chiburi district, showing Isobe/Ishibe groups existed in Oki. Isobe are regarded as the "Isebe" in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|qid=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} section), established with the {{ill|lt=Ama-be|Q446149|qid=Q446149|1=Kaifu}}. Both were occupational be-min involved in fishing and hunting, but while Ama-be were widely distributed mainly in western Japan, Isobe (Isebe) were widely distributed mainly in eastern Japan with Ise as baseRyō Ōta, 'Seishi Kakei Jisho' (Isobe Kōyōdō, 1920), and 'Seishi Kakei Daijiten Vol.1' (1934).. Since the name Ise is also said to derive from "iso" (shore/rock)Many persons surnamed Isobe/Ishibe also served in Ise Jingū rites., links with Ise through Isobe are inferred. Especially because the {{ill|lt=Isobe clan|Q11588086|qid=Q11588086|1=Isobe Clan}} in Ochi District where this shrine stands seem to have been powerful enough to hold assistant district office, it is inferred that maritime groups headed by Isobe were involved in this shrine's founding. The shrine was known early to the center. '{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}' ({{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 1 / {{ill|lt=848|Q30380|qid=Q30380|1=848}}, 11th month, day 16) says it was treated according to the precedent for "myōjin" due to repeated miracles. This is one of the few cases clearly stating why a shrine was ranked myojin-taisha in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}}Only 3 cases (4 shrines) state the reason: the case of Mishima-no-kami (now {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}) and Ikonahime-no-kami in {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}, and the case of Iyahiko-no-kami (now {{ill|lt=Yahiko Shrine|Q195684|qid=Q195684|1=Yahiko Shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}.. It was also one of the four major Oki shrines treated as kokuhei-taisha (myojin-taisha) in the {{ill|lt=Engi system|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engi system|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, but does not appear in Ochi District of the '{{ill|lt=Oki no Kuni Jinmyōchō|Q11420490|qid=Q11420490|lt=Oki no Kuni Jinmyōchō|1=Izumi Province Divine Name Register}}'That text has places in Ochi District where divine names are missing. Also, "Senior Fourth Upper Ise Myōjin" appears in neighboring {{ill|lt=Suki District|Q10920245|qid=Q10920245|1=Suki district}}, so it is also argued that it may have been recorded under another district for some reason (Mitsuhashi Ken, ed. annotation, 'Shintō Taikei: Jinja-hen, Sōki-jō,' 1986).. From medieval times onward it was revered by warrior families, with known donations including paddy land named Aikushida by Omosu Kiyoshige in {{ill|lt=Kanshō|Q1143928|qid=Q1143928|1=Kanshō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1462|Q6546|qid=Q6546|1=1462}}), one {{ill|lt=tachi|Q120676|qid=Q120676|1=Tachi}} dedicated by Sasaki Tamekiyo claiming the office of {{ill|lt=shugo|Q1141023|qid=Q1141023|1=Shugo}} of Oki in {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|qid=Q6659|1=1570}}), and mountain woodland donated by {{ill|lt=Horio Yoshiharu|Q8190645|qid=Q8190645|1=Horio Yoshiharu}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Matsue Domain|Q1017072|qid=Q1017072|1=Matsue Domain}}, in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|qid=Q6756|1=1607}}). In the early modern period shrine land income was 2 {{ill|lt=koku|Q855048|qid=Q855048|1=Koku}}. It was ranked gōsha in Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), and after the war joined {{ill|lt=Jinja Honchō|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Rituals === * Annual festival (July 26) - {{ill|lt=kagura|Q1348260|qid=Q1348260|1=Kagura}} is performed through the night, and a {{ill|lt=shinkōsai|Q123907414|qid=Q123907414|1=Shinko-shiki}} is held every other year. Years with procession are called "hon-matsuri," those without "ura-matsuri." Before Meiji the festival day was June 2; after adoption of the {{ill|lt=Gregorian calendar|Q210953|qid=Q210953|1=Solar calendar}} it was changed to July 16, then later to the current date. * Kumi Kagura (July 26 or 25) - dedicated from the night of the annual festival in hon-matsuri years, and from the eve in ura-matsuri years. Designated as a national {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=selected intangible folk cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} and a Shimane prefectural {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=intangible folk cultural property|1=UNKNOWN}} (see {{ill|lt=Dōgo Kumi Kagura|Q11476336|qid=Q11476336|1=Dōgo Kumi Kagura}}). Because place names near the shrine include Hōjōe-da, Aikushida, and Kagami-mochida, it is inferred that rites of {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|qid=Q4402813|1=Ryobu Shinto}} were once held here. === Priests === In ancient times it was likely held by people surnamed Ise or Isobe; since early modern times it has been inherited by the Hachiman family. In the early-Meiji 'Shimago Jinja Torishirabechō,' they are recorded with "family lineage generations unknown." === Shrine buildings === The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is an {{ill|lt=Oki-zukuri|Q11659055|qid=Q11659055|1=Oki-zukuri}} structure rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1841|Q7622|qid=Q7622|1=1841}}); the roof was changed to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=copper-plate roofing|1=UNKNOWN}} in the {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} era. Other buildings include a {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=zuijin gate|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|qid=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}. === Cultural property === === Town designation === * Honden and haiden - Okinoshima Town tangible cultural property (designated {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 7 / {{ill|lt=1995|Q2068|qid=Q2068|1=1995}}) === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (ed.), 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.21 San'indō 4,' {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1983 * {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Ken'ichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), 'Japanese Gods: Shrines and Sacred Places,' vol.7 San'in (new reprint), {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 ISBN 978-4-560-02507-9 (first edition 1985 ISBN 4-560-02217-8) {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isemikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isemikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isemikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Okinoshima Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Okinoshima]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Oki Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shimane Prefecture]]
